message:
fix(grpc): enforce loopback-only URI validation and update documentation honesty

The gRPC client transport is currently an in-memory loopback
implementation, not a network HTTP/2 client. Previously, any URI with
an http:// or https:// scheme was accepted by validate_channel_uri,
allowing users to construct channels pointing at arbitrary hosts that
could never actually connect. This created a misleading API surface.

validate_channel_uri now parses the authority component from the URI
and rejects any host that is not "loopback" (case-insensitive), with
a clear error message explaining the constraint. Empty hosts and
missing authorities are also rejected.

Documentation updated throughout:
- ChannelBuilder: described as loopback-only with doc comments on new()
- Channel: connect() and connect_with_config() doc comments note the
  in-memory transport constraint
- grpc/mod.rs module docs: updated overview to describe the current
  loopback-only surface honestly

All tests updated to use "http://loopback:PORT" instead of
"http://localhost:PORT", "http://test:PORT", "http://myhost:PORT",
"http://svc:PORT", etc.

Test added: channel_connect_rejects_non_loopback_host verifies that
connecting to "http://localhost:50051" fails with a transport error
containing "loopback-only".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
